item.title: Staten Island shipyard explosion, fire leaves 1 dead, 35 hurt
item.description: One person died and 35 people, mostly FDNY members, were hurt in an explosion and fire on a barge at a Staten Island shipyard Friday.

item.title: 40,000 evacuated in California chemical leak as officials warn tank may explode
item.description: Southern California officials have expanded evacuations in several Orange County cities due to concerns over a leaking toxic chemical tank that they say is "actively in crisis."

item.title: Rubio says Iran imposing tolls on Strait of Hormuz "not acceptable"
item.description: The secretary of state tempered optimism for a deal to end the Iran war, calling Iran's bid to "create a tolling system" in the Strait of Hormuz "not acceptable."

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigning as intel chief, citing husband's cancer diagnosis
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard is resigning as the director of national intelligence after her husband was diagnosed with a rare form of bone cancer.

item.title: Pentagon releases more UFO files: "Speechless after these observations"
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a new batch of 64 files related to UFOs, unveiling a second tranche of records under an executive order by President Trump.

item.title: Some schools are teaching kindness classes: "Makes me want to be a better person"
item.description: Schools teach kids how to read, write and do math. But what about a class on how to be kind? In some places, it is. Steve Hartman goes "On the Road" with the story.

item.title: Kyle Busch was coughing up blood a day before his death, 911 call reveals
item.description: Kyle Busch's cause of death has not been given, though his family said earlier the NASCAR icon had been hospitalized with a "severe illness."

item.title: Total cost for a classic barbecue increases by nearly 10%, new report finds ahead of summer
item.description: Millions are planning get-togethers with family and friends this weekend, despite high prices. In this week's "Affordability in America," Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: What's inside the latest UFO file release by the Pentagon?
item.description: The Pentagon released a new batch of UFO files on Friday, including more than 50 previously classified videos and documents related to findings dating from the 1940s to about six months ago.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as intel chief, fourth member of Trump's Cabinet to depart
item.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard resigned on Friday from President Trump's Cabinet. Weijia Jiang has more on this and the latest on the Iran war.

item.title: CBS News Radio clips of fame
item.description: CBS News Radio is shutting down on Friday after nearly 100 years on the air. In this marathon, Major Garrett ventures into the archives to feature memorable moments from the American institution's storied history.

item.title: Lawyers shot outside North Carolina courthouse, police say
item.description: In Raleigh, North Carolina, a woman's civil case turned into a criminal one after police said she shot two lawyers outside a courthouse. Both were hospitalized. Skyler Henry reports.

item.title: Watch: Pentagon releases 50 new UFO videos
item.description: The Defense Department released a second batch of UFO files on Friday, two weeks after its first drop. These are all of the videos included in the latest tranche.

item.title: SpaceX launches upgraded version of Starship rocket
item.description: SpaceX on Friday pulled off a test launch of its latest Starship megarocket. CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood has the latest.

item.title: Urgent 911 call describes Kyle Busch's emergency before his death
item.description: A 911 caller described the medical emergency Kyle Busch was experiencing while reportedly in a racing simulator in North Carolina on Wednesday. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Summer travel begins amid high fuel and gas prices
item.description: Most people do not think the U.S. economy is good, according to CBS News polling. Yet a record number of people are expected to travel this Memorial Day weekend, ahead of a summer that is showing no signs of a slowdown. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Trump administration to require most green card applicants to leave U.S. first
item.description: The Trump administration on Friday announced a sweeping policy designed to make it harder for immigrants already in the U.S. to get permanent residency.

item.title: SpaceX launches more powerful Super Heavy-Starship rocket
item.description: Perfecting SpaceX's mammoth rocket will be critical to NASA's plans for returning astronauts to the surface of the moon.

item.title: Cruz says Blanche faced "full-on revolt" over DOJ fund at meeting with GOP senators
item.description: The GOP senator said the meeting included "screaming," adding that "fiery does not begin to cut it."

item.title: What we know about new UFO file release
item.description: The Pentagon has released another batch of never-before-seen files on reported UFO sightings. CBS News senior national security correspondent Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Judge tosses federal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: A federal district judge granted Kilmar Abrego Garcia's motion to dismiss two criminal charges on the grounds his prosecution was vindictive and selective.

item.title: Trump's "anti-weaponization fund" faces bipartisan backlash
item.description: President Trump is defending the Justice Department's nearly $1.8 billion so-called anti-weaponization fund, despite heavy criticism from lawmakers on both sides of the aisle. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more from Capitol Hill.

item.title: U.S. prepares for new military strikes against Iran
item.description: At the same time, Tehran is reviewing the latest U.S. proposal for a possible agreement to end the nearly three-month war.

item.title: Why did Tulsi Gabbard step down as director of national intelligence?
item.description: National Intelligence Director Tulsi Gabbard is stepping down from her post effective June 30, citing her husband's battle with cancer. CBS News White House reporter Aaron Navarro has more details.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Sept. 11 attacks
item.description: In his final tribute to CBS News Radio ahead of its shuttering, Major Garrett introduces coverage of the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks.

item.title: CIA director brought paramilitary leader involved in Maduro capture to Cuba meeting
item.description: When CIA Director John Ratcliffe visited Havana last week to meet with senior Cuban officials, he brought along one of the covert operators involved in the mission to capture Nicolás Maduro, multiple people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Rep. Glenn Ivey and Minnesota AG Keith Ellison on police reform 6 years after George Floyd's murder
item.description: Six years after his murder in Minneapolis, and despite passing the House twice, the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act remains stalled in the Senate. Some lawmakers are hoping to change that. Democratic Rep. Glenn Ivey of Maryland and Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison join to reflect on the state of police reform.

item.title: Former Trump lawyer Michael Cohen says he would be "a test case" for DOJ "anti-weaponization" fund
item.description: President Trump took to social media Friday to defend the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund despite mounting resistance among some Senate Republicans. Former attorney for Mr. Trump, Michael Cohen, joins to discuss his case for a share of the $1.8 billion compensation fund.

item.title: 5/22: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as national intelligence director; President Trump defends "anti-weaponization" fund despite GOP backlash.

item.title: CBS News Radio signing off after nearly 100 years: "An American institution"
item.description: After nearly a century on the air, CBS News Radio is signing off, with the final reports airing on Friday, May 22.
